epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: manic/hypomanic episodes with functional impact; differentiate from substance-induced mood symptoms

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: low; diagnosis often after severe episodes
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate due to treatment gaps and follow-up constraints

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist services concentrated in urban centers; clinical diagnosis in general hospitals common
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lithium/valproate and antipsychotics; psychoeducation; relapse prevention; inpatient care for acute mania when needed
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma, specialist shortage, and medication monitoring barriers (e.g., lithium labs) in rural settings
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic mood stabilizers and expanding tertiary psychiatry services
